stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 investment portfolio options. The probability of favorable market conditions is uncertain, estimated between 31% and 42%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: bonds: $75K in good market conditions, $25K in bad market conditions. index fund: $224K in good market conditions, $129K in bad market conditions. tech stocks: $225K in good market conditions, $48K in bad market conditions. real estate: $142K in good market conditions, $18K in bad market conditions. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.36: best option is index fund with EV=$163.2K. index fund is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [158.4, 168.9].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
index fund is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
